_doc: Fixtures for bit-exact kernel parity tests. Regenerate via scripts/gen-kernel-fixtures.py. Baseline matches are the highest we've observed; any regression vs baseline should block a merge.

kernels.name: rms_norm
kernels.note: 1 ULP mismatch tracked to PyTorch's pairwise sum-of-squares accumulation order. If Phase E parity fails on norms, switch to Kahan summation.

kernels.name: matmul_bf16_x_int4block
kernels.note: bf16 output of an fp32 matmul over dequantized int4 (block 32, sym, fp16 scale). Expected bounded by bf16 ULP (~4e-3 rel for unit-ish values) plus int4 quant error; 1e-3 rel is tight for this combo.

kernels.name: matmul_bf16_out_f32
kernels.note: F.linear with fp32 accumulation; rel_tol matches ~1/256 (bf16 input precision at typical magnitudes).

kernels.name: model_forward
kernels.note: End-to-end forward pass on a 1-layer-truncated model: embed → block0 → final_norm → classifier. Reference is PyTorch with the same 1-layer truncation + dequantized experts. Tolerance accommodates compound drift through the full pipeline on short (T=16) inputs.

kernels.name: block_forward
kernels.note: Full transformer block with routing provided (synthetic, cycles through experts 0..3). Reference uses upstream modules with experts patched to our dequantized weights so tolerance bounds kernel drift only. Tolerance accommodates compound drift across 4 bf16 matmul chains + attention + MoE; large-magnitude outputs (|want| ≥ 10) all match within 15% rel, smaller ones within ~1 absolute. For end-to-end parity on final logits we'll tighten.

kernels.name: expert_dispatch
kernels.note: MoE expert dispatch with 4 experts (truncated from 128). Every token routes to all 4 with uniform score 1/4. Reference uses the dequantized weights from our blob (not the model's fp32), so the tolerance bounds kernel rounding only.

kernels.name: attention_forward
kernels.note: End-to-end attention: Q/K/V projections + RoPE + scale + banded attention + O projection. Tolerance is loose (10% rel, 5% abs) because our matmul chain accumulates in f32 through to the final bf16 round, while PyTorch rounds to bf16 between every op — drift compounds across 4 matmuls. Real layer-0 weights from openai/privacy-filter.

kernels.name: banded_attention
kernels.note: End-to-end attention: QK matmul + mask + sinks + softmax + AV. Tolerance allows for (1) 3-rounding bf16 semantics in the QK and AV matmul chains, (2) exp drift vs libm, (3) order-of-summation effects in the V-weighted sum.
